stimulus: "The Memory Vault," an acclaimed science fiction novella by South Korean author Min-jun Park, takes place in an alternate version of Seoul where personal memories are a valuable resource that can be extracted and stored in private databases. ______blank citizens must pay subscription fees to access their own childhood recollections.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The second sentence describes a consequence of the system laid out in the first sentence: because memories are stored in private databases in this world, people must pay to access them.

Choice A is incorrect because "in any case" doesn't fit here; there's only one fictional situation described. Choice C is incorrect because "nevertheless" illogically signals contrast, when the two sentences describe the same fictional setup. Choice D is incorrect because "earlier" illogically signals a time shift backward, when both sentences describe the same fictional present.
